statement.markdown: Given a base and an exponent, calculate the value of the base raised to the power of the exponent. The binary exponentiation algorithm should be used to optimize the calculation.
statement.inputFormat: An integer base and an integer exponent.
statement.outputFormat: The result of base raised to the power of exponent.
statement.notes: The base and exponent will be within the range of 1 to 2^31-1. The result must be calculated accurately.

hints.text: Use the property of exponentiation to simplify the calculation.
Make sure the base is handled correctly in all cases.

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: The binary exponentiation algorithm can be used to optimize the calculation by repeatedly squaring the base and reducing the exponent.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would involve simply multiplying the base by itself for 'exponent' number of times.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use the property of exponentiation to simplify the calculation.
editorial.optimalSolution: By using the property of exponentiation and handling the base correctly, we can optimize the calculation.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven using the mathematical property of exponentiation.
editorial.dryRun: Let's try to calculate the value of 2^10 using the optimal solution.
commonMistakes: Not simplifying the calculation using exponentiation property

functionSignature.python: def binary_exponentiation(self, base: int, exp: int) -> int;
